root: ^Norman: Haa... youngsters... I remember when I had the energy to go out and about all day...

first_choice: ^What did you do when you were young?
nowadays_reply: ^Sam: What do you spend your time doing nowadays, then?
^Norman: Good grief, it's the same old every day...
^Norman: Wake up, eat, take a stroll to the docks, and watch the fishermen do their thing.
^Norman: Can't do much more than that with these old knees.
^Sam: Wouldn't you like to join them?
^Norman: Gah, if I knew how to fish, maybe I would. I'm far too old to pick up anything new now.
young_advance: ^Sam: What was your favorite thing when you were young?
^Norman: Gosh, everyone in this town is a fisherman, but I never picked it up.
^Norman: All my buddies would go to the docks, begging the pros to be apprentices.
^Norman: Me? I delivered the paper every morning...
^Norman: 40 years on that bike will do a real number on your knees, I tell ya.
^Norman: I like your composure, kid. Show me your catch every now and then.
^Norman: I might not know how to fish, but I know just about everything there is to know about the fish in these waters.
